name: Red Sea / Suez Canal
summary: Day 67-68 of non-mirror status — UKMTO MODERATE threat level holding; Red Sea track held PRISTINE through Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First (Apr 29). Per intel.md A10: UNCHANGED — one-line reference. Container freight FBX (Apr 21-22 Freightos): Asia-USEC $3,810/FEU (+4%); Asia-USWC $2,653/FEU (+7%) — transatlantic spike (~50% mid-April) shows Hormuz pressure. Soufan coordination-decay thesis substantially strengthened. PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY (preserved): UKMTO JMIC Update 034 (April 19 cutoff): 'No confirmed maritime attacks, targeting behaviour, or suspicious approaches… Houthi rhetoric continuing without operational indicators.' MODERATE threat level unchanged. Red Sea track held PRISTINE through IRGC Wednesday kinetic resumption, CENTCOM 31-vessel Hormuz blockade-stat, Trump April 25 cancellation of Witkoff-Kushner dispatch, AND Trump's 3-5 day patience window expiring Sunday April 26 23:59 IST without strike — Mansour's 'Gulf state directly involved' conditional doctrine NOT triggered across the most pressure-loaded weekend of the war. Soufan coordination-decay thesis substantially strengthened. Container freight FBX Global ~1,886 baseline persists per Trading Economics / Freightos — Red Sea rerouting elevated. Windward/Freightos: carriers taking cautious Red Sea returns; Maersk MECL service operational. The IntelliNews March 24 'Houthis enter the war' framing is now durably falsified. If 64 days of Hormuz escalation, US blockade, Trump strike-list rhetoric, and now diplomatic collapse did NOT produce Houthi Red Sea kinetic, the operational linkage to Iran is structurally broken or deliberately held in deep reserve.

active_triggers.condition: Houthis attack commercial shipping in the Red Sea
